Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»SA medal hopeful Lara van Niekerk OUT of Paris Olympics 2024

South African swimming medal hopeful Lara van Niekerk has been forced to shelve her dream of making her Olympics debut in Paris this year.

South African swimming medal hopefulLara van Niekerkhas been forced to shelve her dream of making her Olympics debut in Paris this year due to injury.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section

The 21-year-old breaststroker wasnotpart of the eight-strongTeam SAsquadannouncedby the South African Sports Confederation, Olympic and Paralympic Committee (SASCOC) in May.

Van Niekerk missed out on automatic qualification at the SA National Swimming Championships in Gqeberha in April.

Van Niekerk showed promising signs at this year’s SA Championships, finishing second in both the women’s 50m breaststroke and 100m breaststroke final, but fell short of posting Olympic qualifying times.

Last week, Van Niekerk competed at theMare Nostrumseries stop in Barcelona where she finished sixth in the 50m breaststroke final and fourth in the 100m breaststroke final.

Just days before participating in the Monaco leg, Van Niekerk was forced to withdraw due to aback injury.

In anInstagram postthis week, Van Niekerk confirmed that shewon’tbe fit for the 2024 Paris Olympics which are scheduled to be staged from 26 July to 11 August.

The swimming portion of the Games will take place between 27 July and 4 August.

Lara van Niekerk wrote:“It breaks my heart to be missing out on the Paris Olympic Games as 2024 was the Olympics I have worked for since being a little girl.

“I qualified on two different occasions but due to circumstances beyond my control the times did not count. The Olympic dream is not over, it simply got delayed.

“Good luck to everyone competing, I wish I could be there in person but I will be watching and cheering from home. I will be back.”

A post shared by Lara van Niekerk (@lara_vn_)

Team SA’s eight swimmer squad comprises of three men and five women.

Medal hopes will rest with reigning 200m breaststroke Olympic champion Tatjana Smith (formerly Schoenmaker), 20-year-old backstroker Pieter Coetzé and, perhaps, South Africa’s most decorated Olympian, Chad le Clos.

Le Clos will be attending hisfourthOlympics.

He joins an exclusive club including Khotso Mokoena, Ryk Neethling, Hendrik Ramaala, Roland Schoeman and Sunette Viljoen to have competed at that many Games.

Le Clos has won a South Africa record four Olympic medals, namely a gold and three silvers.

As reported byThe South African website, athletes who wingoldwill be awardedR400 000, with the coach receivingR100 000.

Silver medalists will earnR200 000andR50 000, while the bronze medal payouts will beR75 000andR25 000for the athlete and coach, respectively.

Men

Pieter Coetzé (100m, 200m backstroke), Chad le Clos (100m butterfly), Matthew Sates (100m, 200m butterfly and 200m IM)

Women

Aimee Canny (200m freestyle), Kaylene Corbett (200m breaststroke), Erin Gallagher (100m butterfly), Rebecca Meder (200m IM), Tatjana Smith (100m, 200m breaststroke)
